robustness.note: Coder v4 rewrote Tests/BuilderSanity/BuilderSanity.test.goal:7 to use unquoted list literal [1, 2, 3] (was quoted '[1, 2, 3]'). With the unquoted form the builder's LLM has no degree of freedom — three cache=false rebuilds produced byte-identical .pr files and the test passed each time. The non-determinism that bit me in v3 is gone.

v1_fixture_remutation.result: test failed at Store assertion as expected — fixture still catches the regression under v4 rebuild

v3_misattribution.what_happened: v3 ran `plang build /BuilderSanity --cache=false` as a builder-validation step before `plang --test`. The LLM rebuild produced a .pr where %items% = '[1, 2, 3]' came out as a string literal, not a list. plang --test then ran against the locally-modified .pr, foreach yielded the whole string once, math.add threw FormatException. v3 committed only .bot/ so the bad .pr never reached origin, but issued a FAIL verdict attributing the failure to the branch.

summary: All clear. Both suites green; the list-literal rewrite of BuilderSanity is deterministic across builder reruns; the V1 fixture for the json.Writer view-threading still mutation-catches; coder v4 wrote the missing baseline-tests.md. v3 FAIL verdict was tester procedural — my own builder rebuild on a graded fixture produced a bad .pr. Memory updated.
